content:
# Imaginary Conductivity

**Summary**: The imaginary part of complex conductivity, representing the polarization effect and energy storage.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2023-badhafere|Badhafere, 2023]] used imaginary conductivity to assess the concentration of pyrite in carbonate sediment bodies.

## Scientific Context

- [[2023-badhafere|Badhafere, 2023]] Used to identify the polarization effect and estimate particle size.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2023-badhafere|Badhafere, 2023]] Measured via SIP at various frequencies.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Imaginary conductivity peaked at approximately 1000 Hz for all samples due to consistent particle size. ([[2023-badhafere|Badhafere, 2023, p. 59]])
